evidence: Runtime seam composition is repeatedly split into large callback bags plus nil-validation layers (for example importerRuntime with init/upload/provision bags and 20+ missing-callback errors in goseg/importer/importer.go:47-126,97-120,208-312).

Startup orchestration keeps duplicated callback inventories in parallel APIs (`SubsystemSteps` and `MissingCallbacks`) across multiple runtime structs, which creates dual sources of truth and high change surface (goseg/startuporchestrator/startup_orchestrator.go:98-149,392-453,474-560).

Rectify runtime stacks interface + adapter + forwarding methods with mostly pass-through behavior and repeated missing-dependency checks rather than domain-level abstraction compression (goseg/rectify/rectify.go:33-170,233-315).

C2C orchestration policy is split across two runtime/config bags (`c2cRuntime` in main and `c2cModeFlow` deps in system), increasing indirection without a single cohesive boundary (goseg/main.go:127-218, goseg/system/wifi_c2c_policy.go:10-104).

findings.summary: Large callback-bag runtimes create high indirection cost and force duplicated nil-validation logic instead of stable capability abstractions.
evidence: importerProvisionRuntime defines a broad set of function fields (volume resolution, workflow dispatch, ship lifecycle, container/volume ops) and separately validates each field in long guard chains (goseg/importer/importer.go:60-78,256-312).

RectifyRuntime layers interfaces and adapters where methods primarily forward to stored callbacks with nil checks, duplicating behavior across adapter and runtime wrappers (goseg/rectify/rectify.go:57-112,233-315).

workflowRuntime embeds RuntimeTransitionOps plus additional callbacks and then performs another missing-callback list validation, repeating the same pattern in another subsystem (goseg/shipworkflow/operations_runtime.go:23-32,120-159).
main.go creates another callback-driven runtime family (`bootstrapRuntimeFns`, `c2cRuntime`) with mostly delegation and default fallback wiring (goseg/main.go:60-91,152-218).
findings.suggestion: Replace ad-hoc callback bags with narrower capability interfaces per use case (for example, `UploadStorage`, `UploadProvisioner`, `TransitionEmitter`, `C2CModeController`), provide one constructor-validated implementation per subsystem, and remove duplicated per-field nil guards once constructors guarantee completeness.

findings.summary: Startup runtime abstractions duplicate service definitions and validation rules across parallel methods, making contracts drift-prone.
evidence: `startupInitCoreSubsystemRuntime` defines callbacks once in `SubsystemSteps` and again in `MissingCallbacks`; every added/renamed step requires synchronized edits in both places (goseg/startuporchestrator/startup_orchestrator.go:109-149).

The same duplication pattern is repeated for background services (`ServiceSpecs` and `MissingCallbacks`) across core/ship/startram/logstream runtime structs (goseg/startuporchestrator/startup_orchestrator.go:405-453,482-560).
main bootstrap wiring depends on these layered startup runtime wrappers (`bootstrapRuntimeFns` -> startup orchestrator), compounding indirection around startup policy (goseg/main.go:60-107).
findings.suggestion: Define startup phases from one declarative registry (name, policy, callback pointer, required flag) and derive both execution list and missing-callback diagnostics from that single source to eliminate duplicated abstraction contracts.

findings.summary: Transition publication side effects are not encapsulated behind a strict single-call abstraction, allowing duplicate emission in reconciliation flow.
evidence: Within `StartramRetrieveReconciler.Reconcile`, `publishUrbitServiceRegistrationTransitionWithCurrentState` is called twice consecutively for the same `(current, patp, serviceCreated)` tuple (goseg/rectify/rectify_services.go:165-166).

Rectify tests exercise startram transition application but do not assert single-publication semantics for service registration updates, so duplicate state mutation can slip through (goseg/rectify/rectify_test.go:109-131).

Event abstraction in `events.EventRuntime` expects explicit publish calls per event, so caller duplication directly maps to duplicate downstream traffic/state updates (goseg/docker/events/events.go:87-148).
findings.suggestion: Introduce a dedicated service-registration transition helper that enforces idempotent publish/update semantics (single call per reconciled patp per loop iteration) and add a regression test asserting no duplicate state mutation/publication.
